question.text: What is the 'inception date' of a mutual fund scheme in relation to the NFO process?

answer.text: The allotment date of units to NFO investors
The date when the NFO opens for subscription
The date when SEBI approves the scheme
The date when the scheme is first listed on the exchange

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The inception date of a mutual fund scheme is the allotment date — the date when units are allotted to investors who subscribed during the NFO. This is when the scheme officially comes into existence with its initial NAV set at face value (typically Rs.10). **The Trap:** Many candidates confuse the inception date with the NFO opening date or the SEBI approval date. The NFO opening is when subscriptions begin, but the scheme is 'born' only when units are allotted. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A tests inception date in the context of scheme performance calculation; the inception NAV serves as the base for all future return computations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When comparing scheme performance, always check the inception date — a scheme launched just before a market rally will show inflated returns compared to one launched at a market peak. Context matters more than raw numbers.
